experiments.title: Geometric Volatility Index
experiments.summary: 1 Hz team-shape volatility built from spread, team length, inter-team centroid distance, and spatial entropy.
experiments.interpretation: Higher values indicate rapid spatial reorganization and a more unstable game state.

metrics.label: Peak vol index
metrics.hint: Highest normalized combined volatility over the half.

metrics.label: Sampling
metrics.hint: Per-second series with 10-second rolling mean absolute deltas.

metrics.label: Goals in clip
metrics.hint: Count of SoccerNet goal annotations aligned to this half.

metrics.label: 30s pre-goal index
metrics.hint: Average combined volatility index in rows that precede a goal within 30 seconds.

metrics.label: 30s baseline index
metrics.hint: Average combined volatility index outside those 30-second pre-goal windows.

metrics.label: 30s uplift
metrics.hint: Relative lift of the volatility index in pre-goal windows versus baseline.

diagnostics.title: Tracking stability: acceptable
diagnostics.message: 5348 unique player track IDs across 67499 frames (longest track 1745 frames, mean 129.1).
diagnostics.next_step: Review the longest tracks through pans and occlusions to confirm IDs stay attached to the same players.

diagnostics.title: Team clustering: separated
diagnostics.message: 3302 home tracks and 1867 away tracks from 676850 jersey crops (cluster distance 0.298, mean vote ratio 0.89).
diagnostics.next_step: Cross-check goalkeepers, referees, and edge cases before treating the home and away labels as clean.

diagnostics.title: Ball detections: usable
diagnostics.message: Average ball detections per frame 0.60; projected ball anchors 37228.
diagnostics.next_step: Verify that the highlighted ball follows play movement rather than bright crowd or signage artifacts.

diagnostics.title: Field calibration: active
diagnostics.message: 4305/6750 refreshes succeeded; mean visible pitch keypoints 4.6; 92.4% of player detections projected.
diagnostics.next_step: If the minimap drifts, inspect live preview frames for missing pitch keypoints before changing tracking settings.

diagnostics.title: Goal labels: loaded
diagnostics.message: 3 aligned goal events loaded from Labels-v2.json.
diagnostics.next_step: Use the experiment outputs to compare pre-goal windows against baseline match state.

learn_cards.title: 1. Player detection + ByteTrack
learn_cards.what_it_does: Uses football-specific detector weights so player, ball, and referee detection are trained for broadcast football rather than generic COCO classes.
learn_cards.what_breaks: Crowded boxes, camera pans, and tiny distant players still fragment track IDs.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Start with shorter clips, then raise model size or lower confidence only after you inspect churn.

learn_cards.title: 2. Team ID from jersey colors
learn_cards.what_it_does: Samples upper-body jersey colors, clusters them into two groups, and tags tracks as home or away.
learn_cards.what_breaks: Goalkeepers, referees, shadows, and tiny crops can poison the color signal.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Use clips with clear kit contrast first and treat the labels as approximate, not gospel.

learn_cards.title: 3. Ball tracking
learn_cards.what_it_does: Runs a second detector for the ball so the demo is about play context, not just player boxes.
learn_cards.what_breaks: The ball is small, fast, and happy to hide behind players or advertising boards.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Keep the detector lightweight for now and judge it by overlay sanity, not by perfection.

learn_cards.title: 4. Automatic field registration
learn_cards.what_it_does: Refreshes pitch calibration from field keypoints every 10 frames so the minimap can follow a moving broadcast camera.
learn_cards.what_breaks: If the keypoint model loses field structure during a fast cut or tight zoom, the projection has to coast on the last good calibration.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Watch the live preview for calibration dropouts instead of trusting the minimap blindly.

learn_cards.title: 5. Demo first, fancy later
learn_cards.what_it_does: Packages detection, tracking, team ID, and rough spatial context into one believable tactical pipeline.
learn_cards.what_breaks: It is easy to jump to pose or fancy models before the wide-angle basics are stable.
learn_cards.what_to_try_next: Get the overlay and minimap looking trustworthy before you add anything more ambitious.

code_context.label: homography_detection
code_context.reason: Pitch homography detection and reprojection error calculation.
code_context.excerpt:
0811: def detect_pitch_homography(
0812:     frame: np.ndarray,
0813:     keypoint_model: YOLO,
0814:     device: str,
0815:     confidence_threshold: float = FIELD_KEYPOINT_CONFIDENCE,
0816: ) -> tuple[np.ndarray | None, np.ndarray | None, int, int, float]:
0817:     results = keypoint_model(source=frame, conf=0.05, device=device, verbose=False)
0818:     if not results:
0819:         return None, None, 0, 0, float("inf")
0820: 
0821:     result = results[0]
0822:     if not hasattr(result, "keypoints") or result.keypoints is None or result.keypoints.data is None:
0823:         return None, None, 0, 0, float("inf")
0824: 
0825:     keypoints_data = result.keypoints.data.cpu().numpy().astype(np.float32)
0826:     if keypoints_data.size == 0:
0827:         return None, None, 0, 0, float("inf")
0828: 
0829:     best_index = int(np.argmax((keypoints_data[:, :, 2] >= confidence_threshold).sum(axis=1)))
0830:     keypoints = keypoints_data[best_index]
0831:     valid_mask = keypoints[:, 2] >= confidence_threshold
0832:     visible_count = int(valid_mask.sum())
0833:     if visible_count < 4:
0834:         return None, keypoints, visible_count, 0, float("inf")
0835: 
0836:     image_points = keypoints[valid_mask, :2].astype(np.float32)
0837:     field_points = PITCH_REFERENCE_POINTS[valid_mask].astype(np.float32)
0838:     homography_matrix, inlier_mask = cv2.findHomography(image_points, field_points, cv2.RANSAC, 35.0)
0839:     inlier_count = int(inlier_mask.sum()) if inlier_mask is not None else visible_count
0840:     if homography_matrix is None or inlier_count < 4:
0841:         return None, keypoints, visible_count, inlier_count, float("inf")
0842:     reprojection_error = homography_reprojection_error_cm(homography_matrix.astype(np.float32), image_points, field_points)
0843:     return homography_matrix.astype(np.float32), keypoints, visible_count, inlier_count, reprojection_error
0844: 
0845: 
0846: def overlay_minimap(frame: np.ndarray, minimap: np.ndarray, style: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
0847:     inset_height, inset_width = minimap.shape[:2]
0848:     margin = style["margin"]
0849:     panel_padding = style["panel_padding"]
0850:     title_band_height = style["title_band_height"]
0851:     panel_top = max(0, frame.shape[0] - inset_height - margin - title_band_height - panel_padding * 2)
0852:     panel_left = max(0, frame.shape[1] - inset_width - margin - panel_padding * 2)
0853:     panel_bottom = min(frame.shape[0], panel_top + inset_height + title_band_height + panel_padding * 2)
0854:     panel_right = min(frame.shape[1], panel_left + inset_width + panel_padding * 2)
0855:     cv2.rectangle(frame, (panel_left, panel_top), (panel_right, panel_bottom), (18, 18, 18), -1)
0856: 
0857:     map_top = panel_top + title_band_height + panel_padding
0858:     map_left = panel_left + panel_padding
0859:     frame[map_top:map_top + inset_height, map_left:map_left + inset_width] = minimap
0860:     cv2.putText(
0861:         frame,
0862:         "Minimap",
0863:         (panel_left + panel_padding, panel_top + title_band_height - max(4, panel_padding // 2)),
0864:         cv2.FONT_HERSHEY_SIMPLEX,
0865:         style["minimap_title_scale"],
0866:
...[truncated]
